blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: What if walking away doesn't stop it? What if he just keeps coming?

blocks.speaker: Mr. Cole
blocks.content: Then we handle it at a different level. But you let it go there naturally — you don't kick it there yourself.

blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: You'd go up to the school?

blocks.speaker: Mr. Cole
blocks.content: I'd do what it takes. But that's my part. Your part is keeping your head.
We turned onto my block. I could see the front of the house from down the street.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Cole
blocks.content: The guys who were with Daniel. You know what that is?

blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: Diego said they weren't from school.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Cole
blocks.content: They're from the corner. Daniel's borrowing weight he hasn't earned. That tells me he's in over his own head and trying to look bigger than he is.
I hadn't thought of it that way. Daniel looking scared underneath all of it. That changed something in me — not pity exactly. Something closer to distance.

blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: What do I do if it happens again when you're not there?

blocks.speaker: Mr. Cole
blocks.content: What did you do today before I pulled up?
I thought about the choice I made.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Cole
blocks.content: That's your answer. You already know what to do.
